author: The Lee Fam 🦖
title: TLDR; Get the Baby Brezza Air Swirl and not the Philips Avent one. No mess and heats the bottle to the perfect temp every time 😌 ALSO if you are planning to use Dr. Brown’s bottles - the tall ones do not fit in Philips Avent !  If you’re expecting or a new parent, this is for you! A bottle warmer can make feeding time so much easier. Whether you’re warming breast milk or formula, getting the temperature just right makes a BIG difference. We started with the Philips Avent Bottle Warmer. It did the job, but since it uses water to heat bottles, we often ended up with overheated milk and a bit of a mess—water would drip everywhere when we took the bottle out. The dial is based on how much liquid is in the bottle, but we found it pretty inconsistent. There was no timer or indicator when it was done so it would often overheat the bottle. Plus, it didn’t quite fit taller bottles like the 8oz Dr. Brown’s. 🤔 Then we came across the Baby Brezza Air Swirl and honestly, it’s been a game changer. It uses circulating hot air (no water!), which means more even heating and no hot spots. It fits our tall bottles, has a super clear timer guide, and gets the temp just right (with a little trial and error). It’s a bit noisier and needs a flat surface, but we’re totally fine with that trade-off. There are a bunch of options out there, but if you’re choosing between water and air-based warmers, we’re definitely team hot air! 🔥💨 #thisnotthat #newparenttips #parenthacks #babygear #bottlewarmer #babyregistry

author: Britni
title: I have been exclusively breastfeeding for 15 months now on the 23rd, it has been such a beautiful journey, a long beautiful bonding and growing moment.  My journey luckily started out smooth, easy latching, minimal bleeding and cuts, only a few weeks, but as I come closer to 2 years of feeding, my nipples really feel it, I’ve been having to take a little more care and attention towards them, as with my little ones teeth tiny mini cuts have been forming that I truly didn’t even notice.  Our bodies are truly amazing and sometimes the amazement of it all surpasses the pain.  And what am I supposed to do? Look at my not even 2 year old son who barely knows a total of 10 words and try to explain “mommy’s in pain and she can’t breast feed no more” NO, I’m gonna keep going, with the regulated self care treatments I can do on my own.  Because breast milk is liquid gold to my baby, and the universe knows when he’ll need to stop, and that day will be very clear for the both of us in our transition and growth as mother and child.  #breastfeeding #breastfeedingjourney #weaningbaby #toddlers #15monthsold

author: Legendairy Milk
title: Rules for visiting a newborn: Never arrive unannounced. Only stop by if you are invited. Don’t come if you are unwell. You don’t have “allergies”. You are sick. Small coughs and colds count. Keep it short. Babies and moms are tired and need to sleep more than they need to talk to you. Don’t even think about picking up that baby unless you ask. Wash you damn hands. Don’t smell like grandma’s perfume or a middle school locker room. Baby crying? Mom looking anxious? Time to pass baby back. Keep your advice to yourself please and thank you! Don’t comment on Mom’s physical appearance. Don’t expect to be entertained Help with a chore while you visit. Video credit @krusty_kristy on TikTok  Let us know what we forgot in the comments! #breastfeeding #breastfedbaby #momlife #newmom
